title: Aml Alert Case Assignment Flow
description: A risk-and-compliance control workflow that sequences screening, escalation, and approval gates before transaction progression. Focus case: aml alert case assignment flow.
assumptions: Each T1 instance models a single investigatory case and remains unique across the workflow.
Analyst availability (T2) is represented as a reusable resource token that toggles when assigned/released.
Entity link actions (customer, transaction, counterparty, sanctions) are idempotent and reversible according to workflow needs.
Queues and reviewer resources (T3, T10, T15, T16) represent logical lanes and capacity rather than individual staff identities.
Evidence and document resources (T8, T11, T12) are managed as attachable flags; presence indicates usable material for review.
risks: Incorrect or premature finalization: releasing disposition without completing mandatory approvals can create compliance gaps.
Resource contention: unavailable or misrouted analyst/reviewer tokens may stall case progression.
Incomplete entity linking: missing customer or transaction attachments can lead to improper routing or missed sanctions matches.
Over-reliance on automated signals: automated_rule_signal (T9) may produce false positives that cascade into unnecessary escalations.
Insufficient auditability: if intermediate state toggles are not recorded consistently, reconstructing decision history may be difficult.
